policy_statement: TMG will improve island resilience by installing solar generation and storage on islands and considering regional renewable energy sharing.
mechanism_description: Promoting installation of solar generation and storage batteries on islands, along with considering regional renewable energy sharing, supports more flexible local power supply and stronger disaster preparedness.
primary_causal_pathway: Grid operation/infrastructure change → ↑ flexibility & renewable integration (↓ curtailment, ↓ peakers) → ↓ fossil generation → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Utilities, cities, and building/campus operators deploy electrical storage (batteries/thermal storage) and enable interconnection (distribution upgrades, microgrids) to shift load and provide ancillary services.  
2) Mediating physical process: Storage absorbs electricity when supply is abundant (often renewable) and discharges during higher-demand periods, providing ramping/firming, frequency/voltage support, and reserves; this reduces renewable curtailment and decreases reliance on inefficient, high-emitting marginal generators (often gas peakers).  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Lower grid emissions intensity and total power-sector GHG emissions due to increased renewable utilization and reduced fossil generation.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is enabling higher renewable electricity penetration (and less fossil peaking/curtailment) through added grid flexibility, thereby reducing GHG emissions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Storage/interconnection primarily functions to integrate renewables and reduce fossil marginal generation, yielding emissions reductions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While storage can also improve reliability/resilience (adaptation co-benefit) and improve system efficiency (resource efficiency co-benefit), the mechanism as stated centers on renewable integration and grid flexibility—i.e., decarbonizing electricity supply.  
3) Edge cases applied: If storage is charged predominantly from fossil-heavy grids or used mainly for backup diesel displacement/critical power without increasing renewable utilization, mitigation benefits could be smaller or mixed; nevertheless, the canonical mechanism defined here is explicitly “grid flexibility and renewable integration,” which is mitigation-dominant.  
4) Confirm primary applies to ALL instances: Any policy whose core mechanism is deploying storage to increase flexibility for higher renewable penetration has its largest climate effect through reduced fossil generation and avoided GHG emissions.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether a given policy is better characterized secondarily as enabling renewables, peak shaving, ancillary services, microgrids/resilience, or demand response depends on policy-specific design and grid context and is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Storage can be adaptation-relevant when framed as backup/resilience (e.g., outage mitigation via microgrids), and emissions impacts depend on charging source and dispatch rules; however, under this mechanism’s definition (renewable integration via flexibility), mitigation remains the dominant category.
key_indicators: "promoting the installation of solar power generation equipment and storage batteries"; "Consider the sharing of regional renewable energy"; "aim to strengthen regional disaster preparedness"; "Improve the resilience of the islands"
co_benefits: Adaptation/resilience co-benefit: improved island resilience and strengthened regional disaster preparedness (energy security during disasters/outages).
instance_edge_case_notes: Instrument could also be read as a high-level planning/strategy statement (“promoting”, “consider”) rather than a specified incentive; no explicit funding mechanism, mandate, or target is stated. Classified as incentive-program because “promoting installation” commonly implies supportive programs, but implementation details are unspecified.
